query: InfoGripe Fiocruz bulletin June 2026 respiratory virus prevalence SRAG rhinovirus influenza A
answer:
I now have comprehensive data from three consecutive InfoGripe bulletins (SE 20, 21, and 22). Let me compile the complete research report.

---

# InfoGripe Fiocruz Bulletin – June 2026: Respiratory Virus Prevalence (SRAG, Rhinovirus, Influenza A)

## Overview

The Fiocruz InfoGripe surveillance system has published three bulletins in late May–June 2026 covering Epidemiological Weeks (SE) 20–22 (May 17 – June 6, 2026). All three bulletins document a sustained nationwide increase in Severe Acute Respiratory Infection (SRAG) hospitalizations in Brazil, driven primarily by **respiratory syncytial virus (RSV/VSR)**, **influenza A**, and **rhinovirus**. COVID-19 (SARS-CoV-2) remains at low levels.

---

## Most Recent Bulletin: SE 22 (Published June 11, 2026)

**Reference period:** May 31 – June 6, 2026

### Virus Prevalence Among Positive SRAG Cases (Last 4 Epidemiological Weeks)
| Virus | Cases (%) | Deaths (%) |
|---|---|---|
| RSV (VSR) | **49.6%** | 17.0% |
| Rhinovirus | **24.5%** | 18.4% |
| Influenza A | **20.7%** | **46.5%** |
| Influenza B | 5.7% | 9.9% |
| SARS-CoV-2 (COVID-19) | 2.0% | 6.8% |

### Year-to-Date 2026 (Cumulative) Virus Prevalence Among Positive SRAG Cases
| Virus | Cases (%) | Deaths (%) |
|---|---|---|
| Rhinovirus | **32.5%** | 20.4% |
| RSV (VSR) | **33.1%** | 9.1% |
| Influenza A | **24.4%** | **41.9%** |
| SARS-CoV-2 (COVID-19) | 5.7% | 21.0% |
| Influenza B | 3.1% | 4.9% |

### SRAG Case and Death Totals (YTD 2026, as of SE 22)
- **82,544** SRAG cases notified
- **40,259 (48.8%)** tested positive for a respiratory virus
- **3,591** SRAG deaths
- **1,641 (45.7%)** deaths tested positive for a respiratory virus

### Geographic Distribution (SE 22)
- **11 of 27 states** at alert/risk/high-risk level with growth trend: Acre, Alagoas, Amapá, Paraná, Pará, Rio Grande do Norte, Rio Grande do Sul, Roraima, Santa Catarina, Sergipe, and São Paulo.
- **12 additional states** at alert/risk/high-risk but with stabilizing or declining trends: Amazonas, Bahia, Ceará, Distrito Federal, Espírito Santo, Goiás, Maranhão, Mato Grosso, Mato Grosso do Sul, Minas Gerais, Paraíba, and Rio de Janeiro.
- **10 of 27 state capitals** at alert/risk/high-risk with growth signal.

### Key Trends by Virus (SE 22)
- **RSV**: Continues increasing in most states in Northeast (AL, BA, CE, MA, PI, RN, SE), Southeast (MG, RJ, SP), and South (PR, SC, RS) regions.
- **Influenza A**: Continues increasing throughout the entire South region, plus Roraima and Rio Grande do Norte.
- **Influenza B**: Increasing especially in São Paulo, Paraná, and Mato Grosso do Sul.
- **Rhinovirus**: Predominant among children/adolescents aged 5–14 years.
- **COVID-19**: Remains at low incidence nationwide.

---

## Prior Bulletin: SE 21 (Published June 3, 2026)

**Reference period:** May 24–30, 2026

### Virus Prevalence Among Positive Cases (Last 4 EW)
| Virus | Cases (%) | Deaths (%) |
|---|---|---|
| RSV (VSR) | 48.5% | 16.6% |
| Rhinovirus | 24.3% | 16.9% |
| Influenza A | 21.9% | 49.0% |
| Influenza B | 5.1% | 8.2% |
| SARS-CoV-2 | 2.1% | 9.0% |

### YTD 2026 Totals (as of SE 21)
- **77,153** SRAG cases notified
- **37,153 (48.2%)** positive for a respiratory virus
- YTD positives: Influenza A 25%, Influenza B 2.9%, RSV 31.6%, Rhinovirus 33.1%, SARS-CoV-2 6%
- **18 of 27 states** with growth trend

---

## Prior Bulletin: SE 20 (Published May 28, 2026)

**Reference period:** May 17–23, 2026

### Virus Prevalence Among Positive Cases (Last 4 EW)
| Virus | Cases (%) | Deaths (%) |
|---|---|---|
| RSV (VSR) | 47.6% | 13.4% |
| Rhinovirus | 23.9% | 17.2% |
| Influenza A | 22.4% | 51.2% |
| Influenza B | 4.7% | 7.2% |
| SARS-CoV-2 | 2.3% | 9.6% |

### YTD 2026 Totals (as of SE 20)
- **70,211** SRAG cases notified
- **33,245 (47.4%)** positive
- YTD: Influenza A 25.4%, Influenza B 2.6%, RSV 29.7%, Rhinovirus 33.9%, SARS-CoV-2 6.4%
- **20 of 27 states** with growth trend; all states except Rondônia at alert/risk/high-risk levels

---

## Trend Analysis (SE 20 → SE 22)

### Weekly Virus Prevalence (Last 4 EW Window) Trajectory
| Virus | SE 20 | SE 21 | SE 22 | Direction |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| RSV | 47.6% | 48.5% | **49.6%** | ↑ Rising |
| Rhinovirus | 23.9% | 24.3% | **24.5%** | ↑ Slightly rising |
| Influenza A | 22.4% | 21.9% | **20.7%** | ↓ Declining as share |
| Influenza B | 4.7% | 5.1% | **5.7%** | ↑ Rising |
| SARS-CoV-2 | 2.3% | 2.1% | **2.0%** | ↓ Declining |

### Case Volume Trajectory
| Metric | SE 20 | SE 21 | SE 22 | Weekly growth |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Total SRAG cases (YTD) | 70,211 | 77,153 | 82,544 | ~5,400–7,000/week |
| Positive cases (YTD) | 33,245 | 37,153 | 40,259 | ~3,100–3,900/week |
| Positivity rate | 47.4% | 48.2% | 48.8% | ↑ Increasing |
| SRAG deaths (YTD) | — | — | 3,591 | — |

### Age-Specific Patterns
- **Children <2 years**: SRAG driven primarily by **RSV**; influenza A incidence also high.
- **Children/adolescents 5–14**: Driven primarily by **rhinovirus**.
- **Young adults, adults**: Influenza A dominant.
- **Elderly (65+)**: Highest mortality, predominantly from **influenza A** (41.9–46.5% of deaths among positives).
- **COVID-19**: Low incidence across all age groups; growing only in Ceará, Maranhão, and Pará.

---

## Key Takeaways

1. **RSV is the dominant virus** in recent weeks (~50% of positive cases), driving the SRAG surge especially in young children.
2. **Rhinovirus** remains the second most prevalent virus in recent weeks (~24.5%) and is the leading virus **year-to-date** (~32.5% cumulative), predominantly affecting ages 5–14.
3. **Influenza A** is the **leading cause of SRAG deaths** (41.9–46.5% of positive deaths), despite being third in case prevalence (~20.7% recent, ~24.4% YTD).
4. **Influenza B** is rising (5.7% recent), especially in São Paulo, Paraná, and Mato Grosso do Sul.
5. **COVID-19 remains at very low levels** (~2% of recent positive cases), the lowest of all five monitored viruses.
6. **All or nearly all Brazilian states** are at elevated SRAG alert levels, with 11–20 states showing active growth trends depending on the week.
7. Case volume is substantial: over **82,500 SRAG cases** and **3,591 deaths** YTD 2026 as of SE 22.

key_facts: The most recent InfoGripe bulletin (published June 11, 2026) covers Epidemiological Week 22 (May 31–June 6, 2026) and reports rising SRAG hospitalizations nationwide driven by RSV, influenza A, and rhinovirus.
RSV (VSR) is the dominant virus in recent weeks at 49.6% of positive SRAG cases (last 4 epidemiological weeks as of SE 22), up from 47.6% at SE 20.
Rhinovirus is the second most prevalent virus in recent weeks at 24.5% of positive cases, and leads year-to-date at 32.5% cumulatively. It predominantly affects children and adolescents aged 5–14.
Influenza A accounts for 20.7% of recent positive SRAG cases but is the leading cause of SRAG deaths at 46.5% of positive deaths in the last 4 epidemiological weeks.
Influenza B is rising, reaching 5.7% of recent positive cases, especially in São Paulo, Paraná, and Mato Grosso do Sul.
COVID-19 (SARS-CoV-2) is at very low levels: 2.0% of recent positive SRAG cases and 5.7% year-to-date.
As of SE 22, 82,544 SRAG cases have been notified in 2026, with 40,259 (48.8%) testing positive for a respiratory virus.
3,591 SRAG deaths reported YTD 2026, with 1,641 (45.7%) testing positive for a respiratory virus.
11 of 27 Brazilian states show SRAG at alert/risk/high-risk levels with active growth trends; 12 additional states are at elevated levels but stabilizing.
Influenza A mortality disproportionately affects elderly (65+), while RSV drives hospitalizations in children under 2 years.
The influenza A share of cases has been slightly declining (22.4% → 20.7%) over SE 20–22 as RSV becomes more dominant.
Rhinovirus contributes to SRAG growth especially in Alagoas, Ceará, Paraíba, Piauí, Sergipe, Minas Gerais, Rio de Janeiro, Santa Catarina, and Rio Grande do Sul.
